description:
While most necromancers work their art using arcane or divine power, aghori employ the power of their own souls and the energies of the cosmos. An aghori understands that all things within the material world are merely illusions, belying a greater truth. In their philosophy there is no greater illusion than the trinity of life, death, and undeath. For the good of all, they diligently tread on these hollow concepts.

Role: Aghori are starcallers who study the interplay between mortal existence and the stars. They see through the falsehood of life's eternal cycle, which some call the "necromantic triad". This gives them the power to preserve life and defy the iron grip of death. Their mastery of stellar energies allows them to summon tools and creatures to aid them in their work.

Parent Class: White Necromancer .

Class Atavism: Aghoris represents an ancient precursor to modern white necromancers. This class occurs rarely in the modern era, but occupies the same niche as the white necromancer. An aghori counts as a white necromancer for the purposes of fulfilling any non-spellcasting related prerequisites.

Alignment: Any non-evil

Hit Die: d6

description:
Those who seek to find the lost language of magic often forget that it possesses a written form, an ancient and powerful script whose strokes hold dreadful power. If speaking the language of magic causes reality to bend, how far would writing those syllables twist the world?

Parent Class: Protean Scribe .

Alignment: Any.

Hit Die: d6.

Class Skills: The amanuensis's class skills are Appraise (Int), Bluff (Cha), Craft (Int), Diplomacy (Cha), Intimidate (Cha), Knowledge (all) (Int), Linguistics (Int), Perform (oratory) (Cha), Profession (Wis), Sense Motive (Wis), Spellcraft (Int), and Use Magic Device (Cha)

Skill Ranks per Level: 4 + Int modifier.
source: Echoes of Magic — Those Who Listen, pgs. 1–6

description:
Bearers of the weapons and armors of the gods, the astradhari are trained in ancient summoning techniques, letting them call idealized arms from powerful legendary beings, called astras, to their hands. They also develop a connection to the plane of memory for various purposes, like learning how to battle like the original wielders of astras or gaining obscure knowledge. Outside of their mystical abilities, the astradhari can fight as well as any warrior and develop a limited pool of essence.

Role: Astradhari are warriors, and as such their place is in the middle of combat. Depending on their astras, astradhari can fight at range, get toe-to-toe with their enemies, or protect their allies. They are also knowledgeable in many areas, and this knowledge can give them an edge over their enemies. In some lands they are known as warrior sages.

Alignment: Any. Astradhari training is not impeded by one's moral or ethical outlooks.

Starting Wealth: 5d6 x 10 gp (average 175 gp). In addition, each character begins play with an outfit worth 10 gp or less.

HD: d10

Class Skills: The astradhari's class skills are: Appraise (Int), Climb (Str), Craft (Int), Diplomacy (Cha), Heal (Wis), Knowledge (arcana) (Int), Knowledge (history) (Int), Knowledge (local) (Int), Knowledge (nobility) (Int), Knowledge (planes) (Int), Knowledge (religion) (Int), Ride (Dex), Swim (Str) and Use Magical Device (Cha).

description:
Biomancers roam the world in search of new and interesting lifeforms. So do this to find ways to improve the lives of living creature; others for the thrill of discovery; and the remainder to find ways to twist life for their own amusement. Regardless of their reasons biomancers are masters of the shaping and manipulation of life. The use raw akasha with exacting precision to manipulate the patterns underlying the structures of living flesh to ruin, repair, mutate, or create lifeforms.

Starting Wealth: 3d6 × 10 gp (average 105gp). In addition, each character begins play with an outfit worth 10 gp or less.

Role: A biomancer's role in combat is largely determined by the greatwork that they choose to dedicate themselves to and the paradigm of constellations they construct as their expertise grows.

Alignment: Any.

Parent Class: Fleshwraith .

Note: An alt class of an alt class? Yep.... In more concrete terms this means that a character with levels in this class can not also take levels in either the fleshwratih or the machinesmith classes. Further, a biomancer is eligible for the favored class bonuses available to both of these classes if applicable.

description:
Where elementalists focus on a broad understanding of the elements and their effects on magic, the Collective also employs a group of combat trained manipulators known as cruxes. They use cruxes and elementalists together to balance the chaotic magic on new worlds and put it to good use. Masters of opposition, cruxes oscillate their energies between two opposed sources of planar power. They draw the embattling energies of these dualities—fire and water, earth and air—into their very beings. They contain the raging power of this conflict within themselves and release it to achieve unique discharge effects. As a crux grows in power so too does the access to new sources of opposition. While these options diversify, the amount of time that these devastating symmetries can be contained is still very limited.

Role: Raging torrents of planar energy, most cruxes start their careers as close quarter combatants striking with alternating blows of elemental energy. Rarer cruxes focus on manipulating life and death and serve both as healers and necrotic-energy armed warriors. As they advance into their mastery of opposition most cruxes tend to find a specialization beyond these to become manipulators of truly esoteric balances.

Alignment: Any. Many cruxes tend to be Neutral as they strive to balance opposed forces within them, but extremes of Lawful calculations of oscillation and Chaotic surges of swelling powers spread the mastery of the crux's path to all views. Over time those that channel the Outer Planes are drawn toward a balanced Neutral view point between the planar oppositions they have access to if any. These shifts in alignment do not impact the character's crux class abilities but may have consequences for multiclass cruxes.

Hit Die: d8.

Starting Wealth: 3d6 x 10 gp (average 105 gp).

Class Skills: The crux's class skills are Acrobatics (Dex), Bluff (Cha), Climb (Str), Craft (Int), Diplomacy (Cha), Intimidate (Cha), Knowledge (arcana) (Int), Knowledge (nature) (Int), Knowledge (planes) (Int), Perception (Wis), Perform (Cha), Profession (Wis), Sense Motive (Wis), Spellcraft (Int), and Use Magic Device (Cha).
source: The Book of Many Things: Campaign Exclusive Content, pgs. 36–42

name: Daevic Retold
class_features: Veilweaving (Su), Bound Daeva (Ex), Resist Influence (Ex), Bound Mind (Ex), Apotheosis (Ex)
description:
The daevic is a fearsome akashic warrior granted powers by a symbiotic bond with an outside known as a daeva, a being of pure emotion and passion given substance and purpose by the thoughts, actions, and feelings of mortal beings.

Each daeva is attuned to a different emotion or passion, and this aspect granted each daevic their own unique passion that fuels their akasha and grants them powers.

A daevic bond is permanent, fusing the souls and minds of both mortal and daeva into a single being that grants the daevic incredible powers, drastically augmenting their influence and might. The first daevic were powerful warlords capable of standing toe to toe with the monsters that ruled the ancient world, loved and feared as tyrants and kings; today's daevics gravitate towards similar roles, seeking outlets for the boundless passions which drive them.

Retold Class: This class is a retelling of the daevic class. A character cannot have levels in both this class and daevic. A character with this class counts as a daevic for all purposes, but some archetypes, feats, and other rules may not work with the retold version of this class. Such rules that haven't had a [retold] version printed should be reviewed before being used.

Role: Daevics are martial veilweavers that utilize their veils to excel in combat, though the exact nature of their role within combat is influenced by their bonded daeva's passion. Due to their bond, they also gain an expanded pool of skills to draw from as they utilize the teachings and skills of their bonded daeva.

Alignment: Daevics can be of any alignment, though their alignment choice is usually influenced by the type of daeva that binds itself to them.

description:
Daeva were worshipped by some ancient tribes as gods and feared by others as demons. Beings of pure akasha given substance and purpose by strong emotion, daeva have natures that seem almost dualistic but are really just manifestations of the same emotion taken to different extremes; a wrath daeva may be worshipped by one tribe as a stern bringer of justice, and feared by another as an indiscriminate force of vengeance, while a daeva of desire may be seen as a loving mother figure and fertility goddess or a wanton slave to carnal desires. In truth, most daeva are both of these things simultaneously, and circumstance alone dictates how they are viewed by other beings. Some beings drives and passions are so in sync with the nature of particular daevas that they are sought out by those beings and a powerful symbiotic relationship is formed, creating a daevic, an akashic knight bound in a symbiotic relationship with a daeva whose ideals mirror his own.

Role: The daevic gain their power by offering up their bodies as hosts to a nascent daeva who lacks sufficient essence to sustain a physical form. Feeding their essence to these untameable forces can give the daevic incredible power tied to the nature of the bonded symbiote, drastically augmenting their influence and might. The first daevic were powerful warlords capable of standing toe to toe with the monsters that ruled the ancient world, loved and feared as tyrants and kings; today's daevics gravitate towards similar roles, seeking outlets for the boundless passions which drive them.

Alignment: Daevics can be of any alignment, though their alignment choice is usually influenced by the Passion they choose

Starting Wealth: 5d6 × 10 gp (average 170 gp.) In addition, each character begins play with an outfit worth 10 gp or less.

Hit Die: d10

Class Skills: The daevic's class skills are: Bluff (Cha), Climb (Str), Craft (Int), Intimidate (Cha), Knowledge (History) (Int), Knowledge (Local) (Int), Knowledge (Planes) (Int), Profession (Wis), Ride (Dex), Sense Motive (Wis), Swim (Str), and Use Magic Device (Cha).

description:
The first eclipse was said to be a servant of an ancient lord of darkness whose influence spread across the plane of shadow, his influence stretching to affect even the material plane. Legend has it that the servant turned against their master in a battle that left the dark lord trapped in the plane beyond the reach of even the gods. Free at last from their master's influence, the eclipse used the paths of shadow to travel the planes as they willed. Over time, they gathered students and disciples who followed them on their journeys, learning the secrets of their dark power and eventually taking those secrets with them on their own journeys. While the powers and techniques of the eclipse are no longer unique, they are still exceedingly rare, as they can only be accessed by those whose hearts truly hear the call of darkness.

Role: The eclipse is a deadly opponent who can strike from almost anywhere on the battlefield using their occultations. Outside of combat the eclipse masters numerous skills for overcoming almost any obstacle.

Alignment: An eclipse can be of any alignment, but their power comes from infusing their life force with the power of pure darkness, and this power, devoid of light and warmth, often calls to evil and neutrally aligned creatures.

Starting Wealth: 4d6x10 gp (average 140 gp).

Hit Die: d8

Class Skills: The eclipse's class skills are Acrobatics (Dex), Bluff (Cha), Climb (Str), Craft (Int), Disable Device (Dex), Disguise (Cha), Escape Artist (Dex), Intimidate (Cha), Knowledge (arcana) (Int), Knowledge (history), Knowledge (nobility), Knowledge (planes) (Int), Knowledge (religion) (Int), Perception (Wis), Sleight of Hand (Dex), Spellcraft (Int), Stealth (Dex), Swim (Str) , and Use Magic Device (Cha).

Whether this dark lord was a daeva whose powers were particularly attuned to darkness, a powerful vizier who transcended mortality, or perhaps even a true god is unknown, but what is known is that wherever his servant walked, death followed. Legend has it that the servant turned against their master in a battle that shattered strands of the Lattice and left the dark lord trapped in a plane beyond the reach of even the gods. Free at last from their master's influence, the eclipse used the paths of shadow to travel the planes as they willed. Over time, they gathered students and disciples who followed them on their journeys, learning the secrets of their dark power and eventually taking those secrets with them on their own journeys. While the powers and techniques of the eclipse are no longer unique, they are still exceedingly rare, as they can only be accessed by those whose hearts truly hear the call of darkness.

description:
There have always been tales of rulers with domains that are true reflections of their innermost selves. Darklords with black and parched souls who rule over blasted hellscapes. Queens with pure hearts and majestic beauty whose lands are full of health and bounty. Many such monarchs exist in legend and lore. Though myths may beggar them, fisherkings are the easiest kernel of truth that can be gleaned from such stories. The knowledge of where, when, or how the first fisherkings came to be has been lost, but some answers of who and what they are remain. A fisherking is at the core of their being a ruler. Their force of personality is so great as to make people, magic, and even shards of existence kneel before them. Though not always kings in the traditional sense, there is always something majestic about those who call themselves fisherkings.

Role: Every fisherking is an individual who causes the world to change around them through their force of personality and via a power born from their connection to places and people. The fates and lives of both friends and foes are shaped and altered by a fisherking's presence. The mere presence of a fisherking is enough to sap the strength of enemies and invigorates allies.

Alignment: While fisherkings of all alignments can exist, they tend towards non-chaotic alignments.

Hit Die: d6

Parent Classes: Vizier and Witch.

Starting Age: Intuitive

name: Guru Retold
class_features: Veilweaving (Su), Gentle Touch (Su), Stunning Fist (Ex), Sunder Veil (Su), Chakra Disruption (Su), Seal Wounds (Ex), Crack the Shell (Su), Sever the Flow (Su), Immortal Essence (Su)
description:
Gurus train to channel the powers of akasha through mysticism, learning to harness the energy and focus of their spirit to subdue their foes without taking lives. These mystics follow one of many philosophies that guide their mystical practices, which guide them out into the world to gain new experiences and spread their ideals. Utilizing their unique form of combat known as the gentle touch, gurus can nonlethally disable their foes through non-lethal damage and channel this energy into their foe's chakras to apply other severe and debilitating effects.

Role: The guru is a martially-focused veilweaver who specializes in disabling their foes with conditions and nonlethal damage whose abilities are further influenced by their chosen philosophy.

Alignment: Gurus can be of any alignment, but are typically non-Evil. Evil creatures rarely see the value in mastering an art that leaves their enemies alive to fight another day.

Starting Wealth: 3d6 × 10 gp (average 105 gp.) In addition, each character begins play with an outfit worth 10 gp or less.

Hit Die: d8.

Class Skills: The Guru's class skills are Acrobatics (Dex), Climb (Str), Craft (Int), Diplomacy (Cha), Disable Device (Dex), Escape Artist (Dex), Heal (Wis), Knowledge (History, Local, Nobility) (Int), Perception (Wis), Sense Motive (Wis), Stealth (Dex), Survival (Wis), and Swim (Str).
source: Akasha Retold — Mysteries and Trinities, pgs. 1–4

description:
The first guru, so the tales say, was a priest first, one who eventually came to doubt the true divinity of his chosen patron. Taking staff in hand, this priest traveled the world, seeking a higher truth. While pursuing this enigmatic truth, he learned many skills, training in the akashic arts with the reclusive vritra, battling bandits when they sought to accost him on the road, and always finding a way to repay the communities who fed and sheltered him, whether through teaching them one of the many techniques of healing, farming, or engineering he learned on his long travels. When the priest had grown old, and his travels grew ever shorter, he began to spread his knowledge, and his quest for truth, to students and proteges who came to him to learn what he had uncovered. Whether any guru has ever truly found a “higher truth” beyond the gods is unknown, but the quest continues to this day, with warrior-philosophers from all corners of the world traveling in search of it and spreading their knowledge along the way.

Role: The guru train in mystic monasteries learning to harness their life energy and focus their spirit to subdue their enemies without taking their lives. The guru can forge his essence into a weapon capable of disrupting an enemy's essence, causing non-lethal damage and even disturbing their inner flow of energy so severely that they succumb to debilitating effects like blindness or exhaustion. All gurus inevitably head out into the world to test their learning and gain new experiences, shaping and defining their Philosophy.

Alignment: The guru can be of any alignment, but is typically Good. Evil creatures rarely see the value in mastering an art that leaves their enemies alive to fight another day.

Starting Wealth: 3d6 × 10 gp (average 105 gp.) In addition, each character begins play with an outfit worth 10 gp or less.

Hit Die: d8.

Class Skills: The Guru's class skills are Acrobatics (Dex), Climb (Str), Craft (Int), Diplomacy (Cha), Disable Device (Dex), Escape Artist (Dex), Heal (Wis), Knowledge (History, Local, Nobility) (Int), Perception (Wis), Sense Motive (Wis), Stealth (Dex), Survival (Wis), and Swim (Str).

description:
Since time immemorial there have been beings who tend to those around them, blessing their charges with food, comfort, and shelter. These keepers of the flame are the heart of any group they become a part of. Hearthkeepers are not taught. Instead, those who have the natural spark of akashic magic sometimes combine that talent with a desire to serve and assist others. This gives birth to a flame that empowers them beyond what their inborn abilities ever could.

Role: All hearthkeepers heal and support their communities. Beyond that, their veils shape their abilities.

Alignment: Any

Hit Die: d6.

Starting Wealth: 2d6 x 10 gp (average 70 gp) In addition, each character begins play with an outfit worth 10 gp or less.

description:
More frequently than not, evolution results in expansive transformations. Bodies and systems grow larger and more complex, with many more aspects and parts than its progenitors could ever have comprehended. Such is the case with war, where individual warriors have eventually found themselves supplanted by troops and formations and those organizations have eventually been invalidated by vast and mighty war machines. However, such machines are worthless without guidance and command. Helmsmen act as the minds as well as the hearts of the vessels they operate, ordering them in battle and pushing them far beyond their normal capacity. Pouring their own knowledge and energy into their armaments, these sage commanders harness technology to the utmost, utilizing it to assert themselves as unchallenged lords of war.

Role: The helmsman utilizes a powerful vessel to command the battlefield, either from close quarters as an immense sentinel or from range as a destructive artillery battery. Either way, the helmsman is more than capable of adapting his vessel to suit a variety of dangerous situations.

Alignment: Helmsmen can be of any alignment, but those that serve in military affairs typically possess an inclination towards discipline and thus a lawful bent. Chaotic helmsmen are more frequently pirates, renegades, or others who seek to use their mastery of piloting for their own inclines. Helmsmen have no particular favor of either good or evil and exist on both sides in equal frequency.

Hit Dice: d8

Class Skills: The Helmsman's Class Skills are Acrobatics (Dex), Appraise (Int), Craft (any) (Int), Diplomacy (Cha), Disable Device (Dex), Escape Artist (Dex), Fly (Dex), Intimidate (Cha), Knowledge (Arcana) (Int), Knowledge (Engineering) (Int), Knowledge (Martial) (Int), Perception (Wis), Ride (Dex), Sense Motive (Wis), Spellcraft (Int), and Swim (Str).

Skill Points per level: 4 + Intelligence modifier

description:
Machines make tasks easier, more efficient, and in some cases make the impossible possible. One might think that the bigger the machine the greater its capacity, and to a point that is true. However, in the case of nanites the opposite is true. When acting in concert swarms of these microscopic devices can accomplish wonders, and synthesize horrors. Whole worlds can be given life, or stripped of it, via the actions of these protean and adaptable machines. Through circumstance or by design a hivemind has become a living factory churning out nanites and their nanite swarm has become an externalized aspect of their own physiology. Some might question if the nanite swarm and its hivemind constitute a singular entity, two separate beings, or something else entirely. However, it would be folly to doubt that it is the hivemind's will that defines the swarm's function.

Role: The hivemind calls upon a swarm of miniscule machines with a collective will to help them solve problems and adapt to changing situations. At its master's whim the swarm can serve as sword, shield, hammer, or even physician. The interplay between swarm and hivemind results in a collective being that is greater than the mere sum of its parts.

Alignment: Any.

Hit Die: d8.

Starting Wealth: 4d6 × 10 gp (average 140 gp). In addition, each character begins play with an outfit worth 10 gp or less.

Class Skills: The hivemind's class skills are Acrobatics (Dex), Autohypnosis (Wis), Bluff (Cha), Climb (Str), Craft (any) (Int), Disable Device (Dex), Escape Artist (Dex), Heal (Wis), Intimidate (Cha), Knowledge (arcana) (Int),) Knowledge (engineering) (Int), Knowledge (psionics) (Int), Perception (Wis), Sleight of Hand (Dex), Spellcraft (Int), Stealth (Dex), Swim (Str), and Use Magic Device (Cha).

name: Hopeful
class_features: Constellations (Su), Champion's Heart (Su), Life Link (Su), Collective (Su), Collective Healing (Su), Telepathy (Su), Have a Little Hope (Su), Cloud Nine (Su), Faith and Trust (Su), Believe in Me (Su), Stand Together (Su)
description:
What is the biggest common thread of those who manage to survive this harsh world? A hope for something. Family, happiness, safety. All are within the grasp of those who were lucky enough to be born with akashic potential. By threading together the minds of those around them, and gathering together their hopes and wishes, one might produce mighty constructs of pure magic to bolster their forces.

Role: A hopeful primarily functions as a buffer and summoner for their team, providing utility and bonuses that fill the gaps that other members might have left open.

Alignment: Hopefuls come from all alignments, even the most evil individuals have hope.

Hit Die: d6

Starting Wealth: 2d6 x 10 gp (average 70 gp) In addition, each character begins play with an outfit worth 10 gp or less.

Class Skills: Bluff (Cha), Diplomacy (Cha), Fly (Dex), Handle Animal (Cha), Heal (Wis), Intimidate (Cha), Knowledge (Arcana) (Int), Knowledge (Local) (Int), Perception (Wis), Profession (All) (Wis), Sense Motive (Wis), Survival (Wis), Use Magic Device (Cha)
source: Hopes and Feasts - Recipe for Success, pgs. 6–12

name: Huay
class_features: Skin Shift (Su), Woodland Stride (Ex), Shifting Touch (Sp)
description:
Even before creatures had thought, speech, or art, they had envy. Envy of the wings of the birds overhead, the fish swimming endlessly in the deep, the teeth and claws of the wolf. And where that envy meets magic, there is transformation. The path of the huay (pronounced hWAY, plural huayob) is an ancient and sacred art, passed down since the dawn of time by shamans and worshipers of nature. They learn to borrow the special strengths and skills of the natural world around them, shaping their destinies along with their flesh.

Like “witch”, the word huay has garnered a significant negative connotation, mostly due to its association with the huay-chivo, a legendary evil shapeshifting mage. Also like witch, players should not let this dissuade them from departing from the stories and playing their character on their own terms.

Role: The huay can fill many roles, using their abilities for things like scouting, combat, or diplomacy. Whether by changing into a mouse to slip into the enemy fortress unseen, becoming a fearsome dragon to wreak havoc on a marauding horde, or simply using their veils to bolster their allies, a huay is always a welcome addition to an adventuring party.

Alignment: Any neutral.

Hit Die: d8.

Starting Wealth: 2d6 x 10 gp (average 70 gp).
source:
Akasha Reshaped: Huay, pgs. 3–6
Moonhand Press Errata Document, pg. 1

description:
A nexus is a font of planar energy, forming a confluence within their soul that allows them to harness the energies of the planes to defend themselves and subdue their enemies with the destructive powers of creation. Through their confluences the nexus can attune to the powers of all manner of outer planes, even combining the powers of multiple planes within their bodies to overcome any challenges they might face.

Role: The nexus utilizes their planar detonations alongside their veils to destroy their foes through devastating bursts of planar energy.

Alignment: A nexus can be of any alignment, though their alignment tends to share at least one alignment component with the first convergence they learned to attune.

Starting Wealth: 3d6 x 10 gp (Average 105 gp).

Hit die: d8.

Class Skills: The nexus class skills are Bluff (Cha), Craft (Int), Diplomacy (Cha), Heal (Wis), Intimidate (Cha), Knowledge (arcana) (Int), Knowledge (planes) (Int), Knowledge (religion) (Int), Linguistics (Int), Perception (Wis), Profession (Wis),
Spellcraft (Int), and Use Magic Device (Cha).

description:
A nexus is a type of veilweaver who mixes their essence with planar energy garnered from across the planes of existence, becoming something new, and powerful. A creature must be born with the capacity to become a nexus, though only a rare few ever discover this aspect of themselves. A nexus is formed when a creature is born with an attachment to more than one plane of existence. While this makes aasimar, tieflings, and other planetouched races the most likely to inherit this unusual power, any number of circumstances can contribute to an otherwise normal specimen of a given species developing the powers and abilities of a nexus, such as a parent or near ancestor wielding planar magics, being born in a location where the barriers between planes are particularly thin, or other, stranger, circumstances.

Starting Wealth: 3d6 x 10 gp (Average 105 gp).

Role: A nexus is a font of energy, capable of devastating destruction backed by akashic powers of creation. All nexus are capable of dealing damage to their opponents from range, while their other capabilities are determined by their convergence and chosen veils.

Alignment: A nexus can be of any alignment, though their alignment often matches that of their first convergence.

Hit die: d8.

Class Skills: The nexus class skills are Bluff (Cha), Craft (Int), Diplomacy (Cha), Heal (Wis), Intimidate (Cha), Knowledge (arcana) (Int), Knowledge (planes) (Int), Knowledge (religion) (Int), Linguistics (Int), Perception (Wis), Profession (Wis), Spellcraft (Int), and Use Magic Device (Cha).
source: City of Seven Seraphs: Akashic Trinity, pgs. 8–13

description:
Some are born with power, some gain power through study and careful application of intellect, and some gain power through force of will or faithful prayer. Then there are those who are willing to pay for power, to gain what they want by bargaining with beings great and terrible from realms beyond the mortal plane.
Pactbounds are the last of these. They have some inherent power, but their desire for more calls them to make a pact with a planar emissary to gain a connection to that plane that enhances their power and grants them the ability to call upon spirits of that plane to aid them by sacrificing the power of their very soul.

Starting Wealth: 3d6 x 10 gp (Average 105 gp).

Role: A pactbound is a font of planar energy, capable of devastating destruction backed by akashic powers tied to their planar pact, but their true power comes from the summoning of planar spirits and the varied gifts granted to them based on their pact. All Pactbounds are capable of dealing damage to their opponents from range, while their other capabilities are determined by their summoned pact spirits and chosen veils.

Alignment: A Pactbound can be of any alignment, though their alignment will affect which planes they can form a pact with, as the beings native to those planes will not suffer to serve one whose goals are diametrically opposed to their natures, even for the short lifespan of the average mortal Pactbound.

Hit Die: d8

Class Skills: The pactbound class skills are Bluff (Cha), Craft (Int), Diplomacy (Cha), Heal (Wis), Intimidate (Cha), Knowledge (arcana) (Int), Knowledge (planes) (Int), Knowledge (religion) (Int), Linguistics (Int), Perception (Wis), Profession (Wis), Spellcraft (Int), and Use Magic Device (Cha).

name: Promethean
description:
Dark stars that glitter beyond the reach of most telescopes, imbued with a profane intellect that promises power and knowledge to those who would listen, and obey. Forbidden tomes forged from ancient secrets and lost obsessions, locked away to protect and to hide. Ghastly and forbidden rituals designed to warp one's flesh, and with it one's mind. All things mortals try to hide from themselves, lest staring too deeply into the abyss awakens something within.

The mortal mind is a fragile and easily fooled thing, often shrouding itself in warm and comforting obliviousness to survive everyday existence. In the rare cases that shrouds are pulled away, their mind given a full glimpse of the vast madness of reality, the experience can lead to transcendence, obsession, and amazing progress.

Prometheans are drawn from the ranks of such mortals, those too curious, passionate, or foolish to leave well enough alone from knowledge not meant for mortal minds. Ever pushing forward in the search of progress, prometheans embody creativity and the pursuit of knowledge at any cost, even if it requires them to destroy their body or their mind in the process. Prometheans are obsessive in their drive to understand reality and their place within it, especially as it relates to the arts of the body, mind, and soul, willing to perform and seek out the unspeakable and unknowable to accomplish their goals, leaving trails of destruction and madness in their wake. This process grants the promethean great power, often at a particularly great cost, allowing them to tap into the most secretive and hidden akashic records and to shape these maddening and forbidden veils into fruition.

Role: Prometheans use their research of occult, alien, and forbidden knowledge to modify their bodies and minds, inflict their foes with debilitating conditions, and to attempt to alter the world to their whims. Depending on their choice of veils and obsession, a promethean can range from a melee-oriented combatant to a debuff-focused caster.

Alignment: Prometheans can be of any alignment, but the drive to seek that what is forbidden and the willingness to do anything to accomplish their obsessive goals causes many to shirk laws and morality.

Hit die: d8

description:
It was said the order known as radiants were once trained in the ways of light by a benevolent queen. Though the queen and her original followers have long since passed into ancient history, her teachings have continued to spread across the planes, with radiants training new disciples in the use of her powerful healing and defensive techniques, alongside mastery of akashic veils that emulate the powers of the fey, nature, and light.

Role: Radiants most commonly fill a supportive role within a party, specializing in restoring their allies' health by fortifying and empowering their bodies with essence.

Alignment: The radiant can be of any alignment, but is typically good. Few evil creatures see the benefits of specializing in magics designed to empower allies and heal wounds.

Starting Wealth: 3d6 x 10 gp (Average 105 gp).

Hit die: d6

Class Skills: The radiant class skills are Craft (Int), Diplomacy (Cha), Handle Animal (Cha), Heal (Wis), Knowledge (arcana), Knowledge (dungeoneering) (Int), Knowledge (history) (Int), Knowledge (local) (Int), Knowledge (nature), Knowledge (religion) (Int), Linguistics (Int), Perception (Wis), Profession (Wis), Sense Motive (Wis), and Survival (Wis).
source: Akasha Retold — Mysteries and Trinities, pgs. 31–35

description:
Radiants were first trained by the fey princess known as the Flowered Queen, back when she was a force of light and goodness throughout the planes, before the death of her son and the betrayal of her confidantes. While the Flowered Queen is no longer the force of benevolent light she once was, her teachings have continued to spread across the planes, with radiants training new disciples in the use of her powerful healing and defensive techniques, alongside mastery of akashic veils that emulate the powers of the fey, nature, and light. The radiant is a healer who can use her mastery of essence to literally pull sickness and disease out of her allies, attacking it with her own supernaturally enhanced constitution. While the radiant's primary role is to serve as a healer for her group, she also excels at buffing and protecting her allies, and can even learn to transfer illnesses and ailments from her allies to her enemies.

Starting Wealth: 3d6 x 10 gp (Average 105 gp).

Role: Radiants are healers, first and foremost, specializing in restoring their allies' health by fortifying and empowering their bodies with essence.

Alignment: The radiant can be of any alignment, but is typically good. Few evil creatures see the benefits of specializing in techniques that involve investing their hard won power into others.

Hit die: d6

Class Skills: The radiant class skills are Craft (Int), Diplomacy (Cha), Handle Animal (Cha), Heal (Wis), Knowledge (arcana), Knowledge (dungeoneering) (Int), Knowledge (history) (Int), Knowledge (local) (Int), Knowledge (nature), Knowledge (religion) (Int), Linguistics (Int), Perception (Wis), Profession (Wis), Sense Motive (Wis), and Survival (Wis).
source: City of Seven Seraphs: Akashic Trinity, pgs. 14–17

name: Rajah Retold
class_features: Veilweaving (Su), Royal Mandate (Su), Vassalage (Su), House of Servants (Su), Warrior of the Court (Ex), Indomitable (Ex), Regality (Su), Divine Mandate (Su), Symbol of Authority (Su), Earthbound Immortal (Su)
description:
King. Pharaoh. Empress. Ruler. These are the natural aliases of the Rajah. Rising above their peers on both the battlefield and off, the rajah blends their Akashic might with martial prowess to control the tide of battle. No single mortal can win a war, and the rajah knows this better than anyone. With their unique akashic gifts, they bring out the potential of their allies, turning even the most inexperienced soldier into a dervish of steel and death. These few marked by fate are destined to be remembered by history—either as benevolent kings, or conquering tyrants.

Role: A Rajah's primary role is that of support. The Rajah uses Titles to empower their allies by both complementing their abilities and enabling their own. Their vassalage class feature allows the rajah to act directly through their allies, striking down their foes at a safe distance. Through their choice of their path of the ruler, the rajah can further specialize their abilities, whether it be through sheer combat prowess, magical talents, or a mix of the two.

Alignment: Any. Rajah follow all manner of morals and codes, some can be benevolent leaders that wish their allies success and greatness, whereas some are dictatorial tyrants demanding their allies follow their commands.

Hit Die: d6

Starting Age: Intuitive

Starting Wealth: 3d6 x 10gp (average 105 gp.) In addition, each character begins play with an outiit worth 10gp or less.

While their peers in the zodiac class hold that it is the raw power of the heavens that fuels their arts, servants have a different perspective on the matter. Though they also call forth constellations, servants believe that ultimately it is their ardent service to their patron spirits and gods that fuels their powers.

Parent Class: Zodiac .

Starting Wealth: 4d6x10 gp (average 140 gp).

Role: The servant's role in combat is generally determined by their path, and their choice of constellations.

Alignment: A servant can be of any alignment, but often find their attitudes strongly influenced by the constellations they choose to manifest regularly.

Hit die: d8.
source: Akashic Tales: Servants of the Loa, pgs. 4–8

description:
These martial combatants have learned to tap into the record of their own soul stored within the Akashic Record, allowing them to forge upon it the abilities and talents of ancient heroic souls. This grants the soulforge unparalleled flexibility with their combat prowess, effortlessly changing their selection of veils and abilities each day to suit their needs.

Role: Using the might of their weapon and armor veils alongside the abilities granted by their soul allows the soulforge to fulfill a variety of roles, though they always excel at combat; besting their enemies through their martial prowess empowered by their veils and the abilities granted by their forged souls.

Alignment: Any.

Hit Die: d10.

Starting Wealth: 5d6 x 10 gp (average 175 gp.) In addition, each character begins play with an outfit worth 10 gp or less.

Class Skills: The soulforge's class skills are Acrobatics (Dex), Climb (Str), Craft (Int), Knowledge (dungeoneering) (Int), Knowledge (engineering) (Int), Knowledge (local) (Int), Knowledge (nobility) (Int), Perception (Wis), Profession (Wis), Ride (Dex), Sense Motive (Wis), and Swim (Str).
source: The Soulforge: Spirit of a Hero, pgs. 1–3

description:
Before there were true spellcasters, there were those who utilized their veils to create approximations of modern magic by shattering them in a sudden burst of barelycontrolled power. As their practice grew their mastery over spellcasting grew until veils were no longer needed to access spellcraft, with this the art of spellweaving falling out of favor. Some still follow the ancient arts, utilizing shattered veils to cast the spells developed by modern-day spellcasters.

Role: The spellweaver's role is determined by their path of practice and study in shape; their path provides them unique but limited abilities that are fueled by shattering the veils they learn to shape through their study in shape. By combining their path and study, the spellweaver can adequately perform many combat roles, from a more martial-focused caster, to a ranged supporter, to battlefield controller.

Alignment: Any.

Hit Die: d8.

Starting Wealth: 4d6 × 10 gp (average 140 gp.) In addition, each character begins play with an outfit worth 10 gp or less.

Class skills: The spellweaver's class skills are Appraise (Int), Acrobatics (Dex), Climb (Str), Craft (Int), Diplomacy (Cha), Fly (Dex), Knowledge (all) (Int), Perception (Wis), Profession (Wis), Ride (Dex), Sense Motive (Wis), Spellcraft (Int), Swim (Str), and Use Magic Device (Cha).

name: Spellweaver
description:
Essence is the most basic form of magic. Pure, unshaped aether that can be directed as one sees fit, empowering their body and soul with its power. From essence sprang the ability to tap into the akashic record, and pull from it the knowledge of the past in the form of veils. Eventually this study of the record and the magic stored within allowed mortals to tap into arcane magic as it is known today, recording this knowledge in spellbooks and mastering it through study and perseverance.

Between these two states there was a period of flux wherein spellcasters utilized the magic power stored within their shaped veils to cast these spells. This practice, known as spellweaving, eventually grew out of favor and its knowledge was lost to all but the most dedicated members of the antiquated craft. These anachronistic mages dubbed themselves spellweavers to honor the tradition they continue to uphold and continue to make amazing strides in the ancient art of spellweaving.

Role: The spellweaver's role is primarily determined by the spells they choose to learn and the veils they choose to shape. They can adequately perform many combat roles, from a more martial-focused caster, to a ranged support, to battlefield controller.

Alignment: Any.

Hit Die: d8.

Starting Wealth: 4d6 × 10 gp (average 140 gp.) In addition, each character begins play with an outfit worth 10 gp or less.
source: Classes of the Lost Spheres: Spellweaver, pgs. 4–8

name: Sphereshaper Retold
class_features: Veilweaving (Su)
description:
Enigmatic veilweavers who shape and wield strange and wondrous veils that draw upon the myriad powers of the Spheres, sphereshapers are renowned for their skill, adaptability, and single-minded focus on their chosen arts. No matter their focus, a sphereshaper is a boon for any team of dedicated adventurers, easily able to adapt and focus their abilities to fit whichever niche their allies may be lacking.
Through the unique art of sphereshaping, a sphereshaper wields limited aspects of the Spheres of Guile, Might, or Power in the form of Sphereshaper Veils and further modifies those veils with their talent, allowing them to further expand on and customize their otherwise limited pool of veils.
By honing their focus, a sphereshaper becomes a master in a single form of veilweaving. Most focus on one of the arts of magic, might, or skill, but the focus of a sphereshaper can also be directed towards more obscure and esoteric practices of their arts, such as further modifying their sphereshaper veils or allowing them to better change their loadout of veils throughout the day. Some rare sphereshapers even forego much of their overall versatility to broaden their focus on two distinct topics.

Alignment: Any.

Role: Sphereshapers are veilweavers with boundless potential and flexibility, able to focus their training to fulfill any role a party needs through their ability to delve into the skills and abilities granted by the Spheres. Their sphereshaper's focus further hones their role in a party; one with a caster's focus may support their party with beneficial magics, while one with a practitioner's focus may channel their energies towards dismantling their hoes with pure martial prowess.

Starting Age: Trained.

Starting Wealth: 4d6 × 10 gp (average 140 gp). In addition, each character begins play with an outfit worth 10 gp or less.

Hit Die: d8.

description:
Those who practice the path of akashic mastery are a diverse and powerful lot, and few more so than the sphereshapers. Where traditional viziers specialize drawing external sources of essence from the arcane realm and planes beyond, these specialists focus on achieving mnemonic resonance and spiritual compatibility with collections of spiritual energies belonging to warriors and mages of past ages and perhaps even those who have yet to live.

Role: The sphereshaper's role can vary greatly dependent on the veils and mnemonic insights they develop with each expertise they unlock and suites of magical resonance. Sphereshapers most often discover the most glaring weakness of their group or the most pressing need of the situation and shape veils accordingly.

Alignment: Any.

Starting Wealth: 2d6 × 10 gp (average 70 gp.) In addition, each character begins play with an outfit worth 10 gp or less.

Hit Die: d6.

Class Skills: The sphereshaper's class skills are Appraise (Int), Bluff (Cha), Craft (Int), Diplomacy (Cha), Disguise (Cha), Knowledge (any) (Int), Linguistics (Int), Perception (Wis), Profession (Wis), Ride (Dex), Sense Motive (Wis), and Spellcraft (Int).

description:
Storms of great and wondrous destruction ravage the world, some infused with the very magic of the outer planes themselves. Among the outer planes, there is a place where the planes of air and water converge, known as the Elderstorm, this place of endless raging whirlwinds and lightning often infuses the storms of the natural world, empowering them with its boundless energy. When a creature is born during such a storm it is sometimes infused with a tiny sliver of the Elderstorm's fury and power. These few rarely realize the power they are born with, for it only manifests itself the next time they are exposed to the powers of the Elderstorm, most commonly by facing a storm infused with its energies unsheltered. Known as stormbound, these survivors and channelers of the ferocious might of the Elderstorm contain more magic than any singular beings body was designed to hold, and they use this pool of energy to create constructs of pure magic that clothe the stormbound known as veils. As the stormbound grows in strength, so too does their connection to the Elderstorm, further straining their body as it fuels them with essence in times of great stress.

Role: Stormbound are akashic veilweavers that tap into a wild and uncontainable pool of energy infusing their body to form magical constructs known as veils that they can use to shelter their allies, destroy their foes, and warp the very world around themselves, allowing them to control and dominate the battlefield through debilitating akashic effects, or to shape their storm into a weapon with which they can slay their foes.

Alignment: Any. While the abilities of the stormbound require a great deal of mental discipline, leading to many stormbound having an alignment that trends towards the lawful end of the alignment spectrum, these rare and powerful veilweavers come from all walks of life.

Starting Wealth: 2d6 × 10 gp (average 70 gp.) In addition, each character begins play with an outfit worth 10 gp or less.

Hit Die: d8.

Class Skills: The stormbound's class skills are Climb (Str), Craft (Int), Fly (Dex), Heal (Wis), Intimidate (Cha), Knowledge (arcana) (Int), Knowledge (geography) (Int), Knowledge (nature) (Int), Perception (Wis), Profession (Wis), Ride (Dex), Sense Motive (Wis), Spellcraft (Int), Survival (Wis), and Swim (Str).

description:
What comes to mind when you hear the word “thug”? Perhaps a petty criminal, or a muscular bodyguard? Well, that's just the way the thugs like it. Descending from a word which means “deceiver”, the modern thugs are exactly that. Hiding in plain sight and doing the work of their mysterious cults out in the larger world, thugs are an almost unknown force in modern times. The most well known cult was a sect of the Cult of the Destroyer. This sect tried to gather together five artifacts of incredible power in order to destroy all of their enemies, which turned out to be most of the world. They were stopped, and it was assumed they were the only ones of their kind. Which is just how the thugs like it.

Hit Dice: d8

Class Skills: The Thug's class skills are Acrobatics (Dex), Appraise (Int), Bluff (Cha), Climb (Str), Craft (Int), Diplomacy (Cha), Disable Device (Dex), Disguise (Cha), Escape Artist (Dex), Intimidate (Cha), Knowledge (history) (Int), Knowledge (local) (Int), Knowledge (religion) (Int), Perception (Wis), Profession (Wis), Sense Motive (Wis), Sleight of Hand (Dex), Stealth (Dex), Swim (Str), and Use Magic Device (Cha).

Skill Ranks Per Level: 6 + Int modifier.

The following are the class features of the thug.

Weapon and Armor Proficiencies: Thugs are proficient with all simple weapons, plus the hand crossbow, rapier, sap, short sword, tri-bladed katar, and shortbow. They are proficient with light armor, but not with shields.

description:
The power of one's essence comes partially from their very soul and its attachment to the akashic record. Normally this connection is fairly weak, requiring a great deal of effort and skill to improve to the point that one is able to tap it to shape veils. For a transcendent, their soul's connection to the akashic record is so strong that they shape veils as easily as breathing, the record freely opening its knowledge upon their soul to provide them the means to shape their essence into any form they desire.

The sheer power of their soul also bolsters their body, transcending them beyond the mortal limits of their peers, continuously mending their body and their equipment, and allowing them to shape their physical form to match their desires.

A transcendent's veilweaving is wide in breadth but narrow in scope; they have unparalleled access to veils, but the number of veils they can call on at any one time is more limited than most veilweavers. However, their natural powers grant them a number of benefits, such as the ability to shape multiple loadouts of veils they can freely switch between.

Their soul's natural connection to the record also grants them the ability to further alter their veils by modifying them with the concepts and knowledge of akashic treasures stored within it, granting them a variety of small but useful talents and abilities.

Role: A transcendent is incredibly versatile, allowing them to shape their veils in such a way that they can perform any role in combat, albeit in a lower capacity than one who specializes in that role themselves. Using their twice-folded veils ability, they can take on two such diminished roles and use them as needed.

Alignment: Any

name: Universal Servant
class_features: Constellations (Su), All as One (Su), Life Link (Su), Starcaller's Summons (Su)
description:
The following text should be applied to the Servant (zodiac alternate class) to bring it in line with the Universal Zodiac. Abilities that share a name with an existing servant ability replace the original ability of that name.

Constellations (Su): All servants learn to call on the constellations for power, via the art of starcalling, starting at 1st level. The constellations that they can manifest are drawn from a single cosmology of their choice as described in the cosmology ability. Servants use Wisdom as their starcalling modifier. The saving throw DCs for their constellations is 10+1/2 their starcaller level + their starcalling modifier unless stated otherwise.

Cosmology: At 1st level, a servant must select a cosmology to attune themselves to. This must be a cosmology that is tied to their current plane of existence. They must also select any lost constellations or lesser cosmologies at that they wish to access at this time (subject to the customization limit rule). Once they have chosen a cosmology, they cannot manifest constellations from another cosmology without taking the Expanded Cosmology feat. If they spend at least one month in a plane with a different cosmology than the one they chose, they may choose to realign themselves with the new cosmology, losing access to their previous cosmology and constellations and gaining access to those available in the new cosmology. Additionally, they gain a competence bonus equal to their starcalling modifier to identify constellation manifestations that are a part of their cosmology.

See: Constellation

All as One (Su): All of the champion forms of the servant’s constellations have a single shared pool of health points equal to 1d6 + the servant’s starcalling modifier per starcaller level. This replaces any hit points that champions would normally have from HD and class levels, but does not alter their HD total for the purposes of effects based on their number of HD. Any effects that add to or subtract from a champion's hit points (e.g. the Toughness feat, the essence effect of The Boar's champion form, or Constitution damage) are applied to this shared pool for the effect's duration on until the champion that is the source of the effect is dismissed, whichever happens first. Constitution damage, drain, or increases taken by a champion alter the shared pool's maximum hit points while the affected champion is summoned as if the ability score used to determine the servant’s starcalling modifier had been had taken damage or drain, or been increased by the same amount.

All damage, healing, and temporary hit points received by champions are deducted from or added to this shared pool. When the pool is reduced to 0 or below, all champions manifested by the servant are dismissed, and may not be manifested again until the pool is returned to a positive value. If this shared pool has been reduced to 0, it is automatically restored to half of its normal maximum value after 24 hours.

name: Universal Zodiac
class_features: Constellations (Su), All as One (Su), Life Link (Su), Celestial Lord (Su), Starcaller's Summons (Su), Stargazing (Su)
description:
Within the corpus of the akashic arts there are two primary paths that a practitioner can follow with regard to how they manifest the energies underpinning reality. Best known is the path of the veilweaver, which involves calling forth entries from the akashic record and giving them form by entwining them with one’s own vital essence. Zodiacs however, are the pioneers of the other path, that of the starcaller. To a zodiac, the constellations that can be seen in this night sky are more than just abstract patterns, made from stars by the minds of mortals. They are the distinct representations of the aspects of the nature of existence itself. Zodiacs, and those that would follow in their footsteps, have attuned themselves to the shifts, ebbs, and flows of the heavens. Via this connection they have learned how to call down influences of constellations and give them form. In their pursuit of the art of starcalling, zodiacs become conduits for the energies of celestial bodies and phenomena such as moons and aurorae.

Universal: The universal zodiac is a revised and updated version of the original zodiac class. It incorporates a number of lessons that have been learned since the class’ original release. Similar to the relationship between a parent class and its alternate class(es), a character can not have levels in both this class and the zodiac or any of the zodiac’s other alternate classes. Characters with levels in this class count as a zodiac for all purposes, but some archetypes, orbits, feats, and other rules may not work with the universal version of this class. Such materials should be reviewed before being used and some of these issues can be remedied via the alternate class features presented later in this book.

Starting Wealth: 4d6x10 gp (average 140 gp).

Role: The zodiac's role in combat is largely determined by their orbit, and their choice of constellations. However, in general, a zodiac combines celestial energies and their own essence so summon creatures, equipment, and mystical glyphs to aid themselves and their allies, or to hamper their enemies.

Alignment: A zodiac can be of any alignment, but often find their attitudes strongly influenced by the constellations they choose to manifest regularly.

Hit die: d8.

name: Vizier Retold
class_features: Veilweaving (Su), Eldritch Insight (Su), Enhanced Armaments (Su), Veilshifting (Su), Chakra Rebirth (Su)
description:
The vizier seeks to learn all they can of akasha and harness its powers, utilizing their intellect and fierce drive to excel to advise their allies, influence the world, and gain master of the powers of creation. By manipulating essence the vizier can alter their veils to suit their needs throughout the day, or even form bonds with and empower magical items.

Role: Viziers are the most versatile of all veilweavers, gaining access to the largest number and widest variety of veils. A vizier can utilize their veils to fill almost any role in combat, though they are often more frail than other veilweavers without utilizing their veils to support themselves.

Alignment: Any.

Starting Wealth: 2d6 × 10 gp (average 70 gp.) In addition, each character begins play with an outfit worth 10 gp or less.

Hit Die: d6.

Class Skills: The vizier's class skills are Appraise (Int), Bluff (Cha), Craft (Int), Diplomacy (Cha), Disguise (Cha), Fly (Dex), Knowledge (all) (Int), Linguistics (Int), Perception (Wis), Profession (Wis), Ride (Dex), Sense Motive (Wis), and Spellcraft(Int).
source: Akasha Retold — Mysteries and Trinities, pgs. 6–8

description:
It's said that the phrase “the power behind the throne” originates with the first vizier. Trained in ancient colleges in some of the oldest cities in existence, viziers are schooled in the courtly arts and entrusted with the secrets of veilweaving that they can advise and protect their rulers. Many viziers have their training paid for by a noble or wealthy merchant who sees the value in a gifted individual, trusting that their investment will be returned tenfold when they have the services of a trained vizier at their call. While most viziers live to serve, some may find that they have no worthy master to lend their skills to, and still others will seek to test and expand their skills in the wider world. Whatever their cause for following the path of adventure, it is a fool indeed who does not see the value in the advice and power of a trained vizier.

Role: The vizier is a canny adventurer whose understanding of the energy that flows through all things combines with a fierce intellect and drive to excel. The true power of the vizier is seen when he uses his mastery of the mysterious force known as akasha and empowers it with his own essence. A vizier can utilize this ability to create unique bonds with magical items and amplify their power, manifesting powerful veils.

Alignment: Any.

Starting Wealth: 2d6 × 10 gp (average 70 gp.) In addition, each character begins play with an outfit worth 10 gp or less.

Hit Die: d6.

Class Skills: The vizier's class skills are Appraise (Int), Bluff (Cha), Craft (Int), Diplomacy (Cha), Disguise (Cha), Knowledge (all) (Int), Linguistics (Int), Perception (Wis), Profession (Wis), Ride (Dex), Sense Motive (Wis), and Spellcraft (Int).

name: Volur
class_features: Spiritual Embodiment (Su), Brandsense (Su), Brandsight (Su)
description:
The volur has formed a bond with an akashic spirit which embodies aspects of the world based on their primordial forms within the akashic record. Utilizing this bond, the volur can wrap themselves in akashic veils and harness the power of their bonded spirit to inflict akashic brands against their foes, miring them with horrific afflictions.

Role: The volur utilizes their akashic spirit and the power of their brands to inflict blight upon their foes and control the tides of battle within their range.

Alignment: Any

Hit Die: d6

Starting Wealth: 2d6 × 10 gp (average 70 gp.) In addition, each character begins play with an outfit worth 10 gp or less.

Class Skills: The volur's class skills are Bluff (Cha), Climb (Str), Craft (Int), Diplomacy (Cha), Disguise (Cha), Fly (Dex), Handle Animal (Cha), Heal (Wis), Intimidate (Cha), Knowledge (arcana) (Int), Knowledge (geography), Knowledge (history) (Int), Knowledge (nature) (Int), Knowledge (planes) (Int), Perception (Wis), Profession (Wis), Sense Motive (Wis), Spellcraft (Int), Survival (Wis), Swim (Str), and Use Magic Device (Cha).

Alignment: Any.

Hit Die: d6.

Class Skills: The weaver's class skills (and the key ability for each skill) are Appraise (Int), Craft (Int), Knowledge (any) (Int), Linguistics (Int), Perception (Wis), Profession (Wis), and Spellcraft (Int).

The following are the class features of the weaver.

Weapon and Armor Proficiency: Weavers are proficient with all simple weapons and are proficient with light armor and bucklers.

Veilweaving: A weaver learns to shape the magical power known as akasha into powerful veils, which are drawn from the weaver veil list. They know and can shape any veil from this list. At 1st level, the weaver chooses whether they use their Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma as their veilweaving modifier. Once chosen, this choice cannot be changed. name: Zodiac
class_features: Constellations (Su), Celestial Lord (Su), Stargazing (Su)
description:
Long before humanity crawled forth from their primitive caves and began building cities, before the waxing and waning of the dwarven and elven kingdoms, the World Tree grew and flourished, spreading its branches across existence. Parasitic entities and old, dark beings that even the gods feared soon saw the tree reaching ever closer to their dark beyond, and began to slither across its branches, happily devouring the mortal worlds they discovered. To protect the many worlds and races within Yggdrasil's branches, gods, daeva, and primordial beings of earth and fire joined their powers together and wove protective runes with a life and will all their own, endowing them with an eternal purpose to shine above the mortal worlds, helping protect them from the hungry entities waiting beyond the edges of existence. The mortals would come to know these runes intimately, seeing them shining in the skies above their world in familiar and comforting shapes known as constellations.

Zodiacs are drawn from the ranks of astronomers, astrologists, sailors, and others who study the night skies to find their way or make their living, following the guiding light of the constellations. Occasionally such individuals experience a phenomenon known as celestial transcendence, a process whereby study and exposure to the constellations transforms a mortal being into a channel for the power and will of the constellations. Even more rarely, the constellations themselves will choose such a being in response to intrusions by the Great Old Ones, who seek ever to devour the light and life of the worlds sheltered within the branches of the World Tree. Once a being has undergone the process of celestial transcendence, they begin to learn how to manifest celestial spirits into physical forms, sustaining them with their own lifeforce. Those who learn to control and direct the powers of these celestial spirits are known as zodiacs

Starting Wealth: 4d6x10 gp (average 140 gp).

Role: The zodiac's role in combat is generally determined by their orbit, and their choice of constellations.

Alignment: A zodiac can be of any alignment, but often find their attitudes strongly influenced by the constellations they choose to manifest regularly.

Hit die: d8.
source: Classes of the Lost Spheres: Zodiac, pgs. 3–6

description:
Amplifiers have discovered a potent secret of akashic energy: it is capable of acting as a power multiplier for virtually all other known sources of magical energy. They use the power of their veils to increase the potency of their other abilities, whether those be psychic, psionic, arcane, or divine in nature. Many of those drawn to the path of the amplifier view themselves as conduits for the power that flows through them, and see their veils merely as improvements to allow that power to flow more freely. Amplifiers are drawn from multi-class veilweavers who can already shape veils and either cast spells or manifest powers, and they rely on each to fuel the other.

Role: The amplifier's role is typically determined by his manifesting or spellcasting class. Amplifiers know that the only way to strengthen their life energy and grow in power is to travel the world, challenging themselves and testing their abilities to the limits. They eagerly seek out adventuring groups, trusting in strong allies to help them find and confront the greatest challenges.

Alignment: Amplifiers can be of any alignment, and usually follow the norms for one or both of their base classes.

Hit Die: d6.

To qualify to become an amplifier, a character must fulfill all the following criteria.

Class Skills: The amplifier's class skills are Heal, Knowledge (arcana) (Int), Knowledge (psionics), Knowledge (religion) (Int), and Spellcraft (Int).

description:
HD: d8

Requirements: To qualify to become a Ardent (prestige class), a character must fulfill all the following criteria.

Class Skills: Diplomacy (Cha), Intimidate (Cha), Performance (Oratory or Singing) (Cha), Spellcraft (Int).

Skill Points at Each Level: 4 + Intelligence Modifier.

Akashic Legacy (Ex): At 1st level and every level thereafter, the ardent gains new veils shaped, chakra binds, and essence as though they had gained a level in any one veilweaving class they belonged to before they gained this prestige class. This also applies to esoteric chakra slots, such as the vizier's Ring chakra, and for classes which have non-standard bind progression, such as the soulforge or spellweaver. They do not, however, gain other benefits a character of that class would have gained. If a character had more than one veilweaving class before becoming an ardent, they must decide to which class to add each level of ardent for the purpose of determining veils shaped and binds known.

If the ardent does not possess previous levels in a veilweaving class (for example if they used the Shape Veil feat or similar to qualify for this prestige class), they instead gain the Veilweaving, Essence Pool, and Chakra Binds class features, treating their veilweaving level as their character level and Charisma as their veilweaving modifier. They learn a number of veils from the Daevic veil list equal to 2 + ½ their Charisma Modifier (Minimum 3) and learn 2 new veils every ardent level thereafter. They gain 1 essence per ardent level, and gain the ability to shape an additional veil each day at 4th, 7th, and 10th levels. They gain the ability to bind veils to two chakras at 2nd level and every even level thereafter in the following order: Hands and Feet, Wrists and Head, Shoulders and Headband, and finally Belt and Neck at 8th level.
source: Echoes of Magic — Those Who Listen, pgs. 42–43

name: Black Templar (Retold)
class_features: Drain Essence (Su), Black Defilement (Su), Create Husk (Su), Corrupt Essence (Su), Blackened Soul (Su)
progression_json: [{"Level": "1st", "Base Attack Bonus": "+0", "Fort Save": "+1", "Ref Save": "+0", "Will Save": "+1", "Special": "Drain Essence", "Veilweaving": "+1 level of existing veilweaving class"}, {"Level": "2nd", "Base Attack Bonus": "+1", "Fort Save": "+1", "Ref Save": "+1", "Will Save": "+1", "Special": "Black Defilement", "Veilweaving": "+1 level of existing veilweaving class"}, {"Level": "3rd", "Base Attack Bonus": "+2", "Fort Save": "+2", "Ref Save": "+1", "Will Save": "+2", "Special": "Create Husk", "Veilweaving": "+1 level of existing veilweaving class"}, {"Level": "4th", "Base Attack Bonus": "+3", "Fort Save": "+2", "Ref Save": "+1", "Will Save": "+2", "Special": "Corrupt Essence", "Veilweaving": "+1 level of existing veilweaving class"}, {"Level": "5th", "Base Attack Bonus": "+3", "Fort Save": "+3", "Ref Save": "+2", "Will Save": "+3", "Special": "Blackened Soul", "Veilweaving": "+1 level of existing veilweaving class"}]
description:
Black templars are blights upon the living. Training in dark techniques that allow them to steal the life-force of others and transform it into akashic energy they can turn to their own purposes, black templars are reviled as vampires and necromancers by the unenlightened. The fear they inspire in those who know of their true nature is even more terrible, as those who practice the akashic arts are particularly vulnerable to a templar's powers; when a black templar's wrath is turned against such a target, they can deplete not only their life, but their essence, draining them until nothing is left but an unliving husk under the templar's control.

Black templars can come from any origin, but usually their ranks are filled with veilweavers and others whose knowledge of akashic energy leads them down a darkened path.

Role: Black templars must touch their victims in order to exercise their powers, and as a result are often found at the forefront of battle, relying on their akashic abilities to protect them while they decimate the opposition.

Alignment: Black templars are never of good alignment, as the first act in awakening their powers is to drain a sentient being to death, using that dark act to create a hungering hollowness in their own being that acts as a source of power.

Hit Die: d8.

Requirements: To qualify to become a Black Templar (Retold), a character must fulfill all the following criteria.

description:
These strange alchemists have mastered the art of infusing blood with akashic power, allowing them to shape the esoteric Blood veil. Using their abilities they often prove to be dangerous and wild combatants driven by a passion for discovery, providing doses of their infused blood to allies to bolster their strengths as well.

Role: Using the aggressive power of their infused blood, bloodfusers often find themselves in the thick of combat, dismantling their foes with the fury provided by their magic. Using their ability to craft infused blood, they can also make for profitable alchemists outside of combat.

Alignment: Bloodfusers can be of any alignment, but the dangerous and unpredictable nature of the studies and experimentation required to master the art best suits those of a Chaotic alignment.

Hit Dice: d8

Requirements: To qualify to become a Bloodfuser (Retold), a character must fulfill all the following criteria.

Class Skills: The bloodfuser’s class skills are Appraise (Int), Craft (Int), Fly (Dex), Knowledge (all) (Int), Linguistics (Int), Profession (Wis), and Spellcraft (Int).

description:
Cerapter heirs combine within themselves the greatest of the myths attributed to equine kind. They have the boundless strength of the earth, shining magic, and wings that allow them to gallop upon the heavens. Beyond even these gifts they have natural charm and grace that draws others to them.

A/N: “Cerapter”- a winged unicorn. This word is a portmanteau combining the Greek words ceros meaning "horn" and pteros meaning "wing."

Role: A cerapter heir's role is determined by their original akashic class. Cerapter heirs make the choice to forsake a portion of their studies and training to embrace a greater destiny that awaits them.

Alignment: Cerapter heir's can be of any alignment. Even the brightest light can fall to darkness and even the darkest heart can true friendship in the depths of depravity.

Hit Die: d6

Requirements: To qualify to become a Cerapter Heir, a character must fulfill all the following criteria.
source: Apocryphal Akasha: Fable Ponies / Poison, pgs. 3–4

description:
The lordly magic of title veils can gain greater power if the one using them considers themself true nobility. As the distance between them and their entitled grows, so does the power of their titles. But this magic becomes tainted as they dirty their hands by mingling with the common folk. Those who follow this path are hedonists.

Hit Die: d6

Requirements: To qualify to become a Hedonist, a character must fulfill all the following criteria.

Class Skills: The hedonist's class skills are Knowledge (arcana) (Int), and Knowledge (nobility) (Int).

Skill Ranks per Level: 6 + Intelligence modifier.

Weapon and Armor Proficiences: Hedonists gain no proficiency with any weapon or armor.

description:
A confluence of seasons is a rare occurrence, bringing unique forms of weather and particularly impressive displays of nature's ferocity to the realms. Heralds normally dedicate themselves to a single season, but those who experience these rare times when the seasons interact find their experience changed, dedicating themselves anew to understand and master all of the seasons rather than only one.

Role: Heralds of the confluence give up some of their veilweaving ability to instead focus on their storms above all, allowing them to change their attunement to their season with some effort. Utilizing their ability to alter their season, a herald of the confluence can utilize their storms to perform a number of roles, most commonly either in the support, control, or damage-dealing roles.

Alignment: Heralds of the confluence embrace all seasons and the boons and blights brought by each. These enigmatic veilweavers tend to be neutral in alignment, though goo-daligned or evil-aligned heralds that focus entirely on the bolstering or destructive elements of the seasons are not unheard of.

Hit Die: d6

Requirements: To qualify to become a Herald of the Confluence, a character must fulfill all the following criteria.

Class Skills: A herald of the confluence gains no additional class skills.

Unlike other monster classes, the incarnate veil class can be taken by a living veil of any level who meets the classes prerequisites, rather than requiring a living veil to take it as their initial character level. It otherwise follows the normal rules for monster classes. When taking levels in a monster class, the character treats their monster class and one other class of their choice as their favored classes.

description:
Thundering through the battlefield with glowing essence and shining steel, the kipchak is a force to be reckoned with. Clad in mystical veils, they walk the path of war with wellearned arrogance, knowing that few foes can stand up to their akashic might.

Hit Die: d10

Requirements: To qualify to become a Kipchak, a character must fulfill all the following criteria.

Class Skills: The kipchak's class skills (and the key ability for each skill) are Acrobatics (Dex), Diplomacy (Cha), Intimidate (Cha), Knowledge (arcana) (Int), Spellcraft (Int), and Stealth (Dex).

Skill Points per Level: 4 + Int modifier.

Weapons and Armor Proficiency: The kipchak gains no new weapon or armor proficiencies.

name: Ringbearer
class_features: Ring Empowerment (Su), Bearer of Rings (Su)
description:
Wielders of the esoteric form of akasha known as Ring veils, ringbearers are members of an ancient and mystical order that have mastered this long-forgotten art. Using their unique skills, these veilweavers roam the world crafting and providing rings of power to those who need them most, often showing up at the most opportune and fateful moments to shape the tides of history.

Role: Using their Ring veils, the ringbearer brings to bear potent magical abilities that can benefit their allies and allow them to tactically control the battlefield. Using their ability to craft and provide rings, they can also make a great deal of money as crafters, or even further empower their allies.

Alignment: Ringbearers can be of any alignment, but the long study required to master the art often leads to those of Lawful alignments who possess patience and self-discipline to succeed.

Hit Dice: d6

Requirements: To qualify to become a Ringbearer, a character must fulfill all the following criteria.

Class Skills: The ringbearer’s class skills are Appraise (Int), Craft (Int), Fly (Dex), Knowledge (all) (Int), Linguistics (Int), Profession (Wis), and Spellcraft (Int).

description:
Some seek corruption for the power it might grant themselves, others harness the powers of corruption solely for more sadistic ends; to corrupt the very bodies and souls of the foes they face.

Alignment: Drawn to the power granted by corruption, sowers of corruption are usually Neutral, if they seek this path of corruption to empower themself, or Evil, if they seek the path of corruption to lay waste to their enemies. Rarely a Good-aligned character can fall towards the path of corruption in a vain attempt to channel its dark energies for seemingly benevolent purposes.

Hit Die: d10.

Requirements: To qualify to become a Sower of Corruption, a character must fulfill all the following criteria.

Class Skills: The sower of corruption’s class skills are Intimidate, Knowledge (Arcana), Knowledge (Religion), and Stealth.

Skill Ranks Per Level: 2 + Int modifier.

Role: The veilshifter is a versatile veilweaver that uses their mastery and knowledge of a variety of veils to solve any problem. They can easily change their strategy during combat using their veilshifting ability to overcome an enemies defenses, and more readily change their veils to solve problems outside of combat using their Shift Veil feat.

Alignment: Veilshifters can be of any alignment, through the intense meditation required to achieve the skills of veilshifting tends to favor those of nonchaotic alignments.

description:
Stormbound are incredibly rare amongst veilweavers, though knowledge of their abilities is not. Many seek to emulate these abilities through an application of their own power, often with limited success. The rare few who succeed often rest on their laurels, using their gained ability for practical purposes, whereas some continue their training, delving into the knowledge of stormshaping for the purposes of war. These storm warriors use their power to shape Storm veils to dominate their foes and control the battlefield, becoming embodiments of the destructive forces of nature.

Role: The storm warrior is the destructive power of the storm given living shape, using their might and powers to control the battlefield and dominate their foes.

Alignment: Storm warriors can be of any alignment, though their desire for destruction often causes many of those who seek this path to trend towards evil or chaos.

Hit Die: d10.

Requirements: To qualify to become a Storm Warrior, a character must fulfill all the following criteria.

Class Skills: The storm warrior’s class skills are Intimidate (Cha), Knowledge (arcana) (Int), Knowledge (geography) (Int), Knowledge (nature) (Int), and Survival (Wis)

Hovering as living examples of each Virtue's trinity focus the angels are, not surprisingly, confused with actual celestial beings when the Daestari first arrive on a new world.
